name: Marine War Risk Insurance
summary: Day 63 Thursday. AWRP ~1% INTACT FOR NEGOTIATED TRANSITS (1.5%-3% Strait transit, 5% for US/UK/Israeli vessels per Lloyd's List/Strauss Center); $7.5M-$14M PER VOYAGE. Brent closes $118-120 (+6-8% Apr 29, Day 1 of $115-sustained CONFIRMED) on Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First. War risk insurance is LAGGING INDICATOR: reprices only on operational confirmation (Hormuz reopening, Iran kinetic cessation, formal IDF combat-readiness stand-down, Sadara JV resumption), NOT on Brent moves or Trump rhetoric. Joint War Committee MAINTAINS entire Persian Gulf 'high-risk' zone designation. 329 vessels in Persian Gulf = ~$352B coverage gap that private markets are no longer providing. DFC $40B reinsurance partnership is policy backstop. Hormuz transit cost stack ~$6-10M per voyage all-in. Sequential reinsurance chain rebuild required ('cannot be compressed by political will' per Lloyd's). PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): Brent gapped -4.6% to $100.47 on Sunday Globex / Monday Asia open — Branch 3 (positioning) defeats Branch 1 (structural break) and Branch 2 (theatrical band). War risk insurance Persian Gulf voyages: $10-14M per voyage UNCHANGED; broad rates 0.8-1.5%; Hull war 0.8-1.5%; US/UK/Israeli flags 3x base; current 4-6x baseline (pre-crisis 0.25%). 329 vessels in Persian Gulf = ~$352B coverage gap that private markets are no longer providing. DFC $40B reinsurance partnership is policy backstop. Hormuz transit cost stack ~$6-10M per voyage all-in. Joint War Committee MAINTAINS entire Persian Gulf 'high-risk' zone designation — has not lifted on Branch 3 paper repricing. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]

current.cover_current: $10-14M per Persian Gulf voyage; broad rates 0.8-1.5%; US/UK/Israeli flags 3x; some easing to 0.35-0.45% on broader MEG routes. 329 vessels = ~$352B coverage gap.
